summaryText: The speaker opened by expressing a deep weariness with the world's sickness—its hatred, divisiveness, brokenness, and corruption. He framed this feeling not as despair but as a divine prompt for believers to step into their purpose 'for such a time as this.' The central message was a call to personal and corporate revival as the prescription for a sick world, anchored in 2 Chronicles 7:14. The sermon emphasized that healing starts not with political change but with God's people humbling themselves, praying, seeking God's face, and turning from wicked ways. A significant portion of the teaching focused on the difference between seeking God's hand (for blessings and provision) and seeking His face (for intimate relationship). Using Matthew 6:33, the speaker challenged the congregation to seek God's kingdom first, promising that everything else would be added. He outlined three outcomes of seeking God's face: it produces true intimacy in a world of counterfeit connections, it brings genuine inner transformation that can't be faked, and it releases the revival we desperately need. The message concluded with a passionate call for a move of God beyond programs and an invitation for salvation.
mainPoints: Healing for our sick world begins with God's people fulfilling the conditions of 2 Chronicles 7:14: humility, prayer, seeking God, and repentance.
We must shift from seeking God's hand (for what He can do) to seeking His face (for intimate relationship), which is the command of Matthew 6:33.
Seeking God's face produces intimacy with Him, brings authentic inner transformation, and is the key to releasing true revival.

versesMentioned.text: if my people, who are called by my name, will humble themselves and pray and seek my face and turn from their wicked ways, then I will hear from heaven, and I will forgive their sin and will heal their land.
But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.
When Moses came down from Mount Sinai with the two tablets of the covenant law in his hands, he was not aware that his face was radiant because he had spoken with the Lord.

Every day they continued to meet together in the temple courts. They broke bread in their homes and ate together with glad and sincere hearts, praising God and enjoying the favor of all the people. And the Lord added to their number daily those who were being saved.

Monday.scripture: 2 Chronicles 7:14 (NIV) - 'if my people... will humble themselves and pray and seek my face and turn from their wicked ways...'
Monday.action: Identify one area of pride in your life today and consciously choose a humble response.
Monday.prayer: God, search my heart. Reveal any pride and help me to humble myself before You and others.

Tuesday.scripture: Matthew 6:33 (NIV) - 'But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness...'
Tuesday.action: Before checking your phone or starting your day's tasks, spend 5 minutes in prayer, offering your day to God's purposes.
Tuesday.prayer: Lord, I choose to seek You first today. Align my desires and actions with Your kingdom.

Wednesday.scripture: Exodus 34:29 (NIV) - '...his face was radiant because he had spoken with the Lord.'
Wednesday.action: Spend time in worship or quiet reflection, asking God to change you from the inside out, not just for external behavior modification.
Wednesday.prayer: Holy Spirit, transform me as I spend time in Your presence. Let Your glory be reflected in my life.

Thursday.scripture: Acts 2:46-47 (NIV) - 'Every day they continued to meet together...'
Thursday.action: Intentionally connect with one other believer today, whether through a text, call, or meeting, to encourage their faith.
Thursday.prayer: God, help me to be Your church outside the building. Use me to encourage and build up another member of Your family today.

Friday.scripture: 2 Chronicles 7:14 (NIV) - '...and turn from their wicked ways...'
Friday.action: In a moment of honesty with God, confess one specific 'wicked way' (any thought or action that goes against His word) and ask for His strength to turn from it.
Friday.prayer: Father, I confess my sin to You. Forgive me and give me the power of Your Spirit to turn away from it and walk in Your ways.

Saturday.scripture: Matthew 6:33 (NIV) - '...and all these things will be given to you as well.'
Saturday.action: Write down one worry or need you've been carrying. Physically or symbolically place it before God, and choose to trust Him to provide as you seek Him.
Saturday.prayer: Jesus, I bring my need to You. I trust that as I seek You first, You will provide all that I need according to Your perfect will.
